studentInfo.name: Arjun Sharma
studentInfo.familyBackground: First-generation student; father is a small business owner.
studentInfo.fieldOfInterest: Computer Science with a focus on AI/ML
studentInfo.personalSummary: Arjun is a highly motivated student from Indore who has taught himself Python and basic web development despite limited resources at school. He shows immense potential in technical problem-solving but needs to bridge the gap in international exposure and standardized testing. His dream is to build AI solutions for agricultural efficiency in India.

realityCheck.reasonWhy: Your technical skills are strong for your age, but your lack of formal extracurriculars and the competitive nature of CS for Indian students means you need a very high SAT score to stand out to top universities. Currently, your profile is strong for academics but thin on the 'holistic' side that US colleges love.
realityCheck.whatChanges: A 1500+ SAT score and completing 2 high-impact open-source projects or a research paper will move you from Realistic to a Strong Candidate for mid-tier US colleges like Purdue or UIUC.

strengths: Self-taught technical proficiency in Python and React
Strong academic record in Math and Physics (95%+ predicted)
Resilience and ability to learn with limited resources
Clear vision for future career in AI and social impact
weaknesses: Lack of international standardized test scores (SAT/IELTS)
Zero formal extracurricular achievements or club leadership
Limited community service or visible impact outside school
Lack of exposure to international application processes
profileAssessment.meansForAdmission: Admissions officers will see a 'diamond in the rough' — a student with high raw intelligence but lower 'polish'. They will value your self-starter attitude but will worry if you can handle the rigorous social and academic environment of a top global campus without prior exposure.
profileAssessment.canBeChanged: SAT scores, extracurricular projects, personal statement quality, and English proficiency certification.
profileAssessment.cannotBeChanged: High school location (Indore), family income bracket, and 10th-grade board marks.

reach.name: Purdue University
reach.why: Top-tier CS program with a focus on research that aligns with your AI interests.

reach.name: University of Waterloo
reach.why: Excellent co-op opportunities that would provide the international work experience you lack.

reach.name: Georgia Institute of Technology
reach.why: Very strong in AI/ML; great for self-taught students with high technical skills.

reach.name: National University of Singapore
reach.why: Closer to home, extremely prestigious, and values strong academic foundations like yours.

reach.name: University of Edinburgh
reach.why: One of the best in Europe for AI; values the deep technical interest you've shown.

realistic.name: University of Massachusetts Amherst
realistic.why: Excellent CS rankings and slightly more achievable than top Ivies.

realistic.name: Arizona State University
realistic.why: Great for international students; generous with scholarships for high GPA students.

realistic.name: University of British Columbia
realistic.why: Strong academic focus and a very diverse student body.

realistic.name: TU Delft
realistic.why: Incredible value for money; very high standards but achievable with your grades.

realistic.name: Texas A&M University
realistic.why: Strong engineering culture and good job prospects in tech hubs.

safety.name: Penn State University
safety.why: Solid program where your current grades make you a very safe candidate.

safety.name: University of Texas at Dallas
safety.why: Fast-growing tech scene; very safe with your predicted scores.

safety.name: York University
safety.why: Good program in Toronto; very achievable admission standards.

safety.name: Michigan State University
safety.why: Strong international support and good safety for high-achieving Indian students.

safety.name: University of New South Wales
safety.why: Great alternative; very high acceptance rate for students with 90%+ in 12th.

scholarships.name: Tata Scholarship for Cornell
scholarships.why: Specifically for Indian students with high need and high merit.

scholarships.name: ASU New American University Scholarship
scholarships.why: Automatic consideration based on your high GPA.

scholarships.name: Lester B. Pearson International Scholarship
scholarships.why: For exceptional international students applying to UofT.

scholarships.name: UMass Amherst Chancellor's Award
scholarships.why: Merit-based award for high-achieving international applicants.

extracurriculars.name: Contribute to a major Open Source project
extracurriculars.why: Shows you can work in a global team and have professional-level coding skills.
extracurriculars.how: Start with 'Good First Issues' on GitHub repositories like Django or TensorFlow. Focus on fixing documentation first, then code.

extracurriculars.name: Start a Coding Club in your school/neighborhood
extracurriculars.why: Shows leadership and initiative, which is currently missing from your profile.
extracurriculars.how: Gather 5 friends, pick a curriculum (like CS50), and meet weekly. Document the progress on a blog.

extracurriculars.name: Build a 'Problem Solver' project for Indore
extracurriculars.why: Connects your CS interest with your local community (Tier 2 city context).
extracurriculars.how: Create a simple app that helps local farmers or small shops. Even a basic inventory tracker shows real-world impact.

extracurriculars.name: Write 5 Technical Blog Posts on Medium/Dev.to
extracurriculars.why: Demonstrates communication skills and depth of knowledge in AI/ML.
extracurriculars.how: Explain what you learned while teaching yourself Python. Share your unique perspective as a student from Indore.

extracurriculars.name: Compete in Google Hash Code or Codeforces
extracurriculars.why: Provides a globally recognized benchmark for your coding speed and logic.
extracurriculars.how: Participate in weekly contests. Reaching 'Specialist' rank on Codeforces is a massive profile booster.

actionPlan.actions: Register for Khan Academy; take first SAT diagnostic; spend 1 hour daily on vocabulary.
Complete 4 full practice tests; focus on Writing & Language section strategy.
Take the SAT; start researching 5 specific professors at your Reach universities.
Start your local Coding Club; build the first version of your 'Problem Solver' app.
Apply for remote internships or start contributing 10 hours/week to Open Source.
Refine your list based on SAT scores; attend virtual tours of your top 5 choices.
Write 10 drafts of your Common App 'Personal Statement'; focus on your Indore journey.
Get feedback on essays from teachers; write 'Why This College' supplements for Reach schools.
Retake SAT if below 1500; take IELTS (aim for 7.5+ band).
Submit Early Action/Decision applications to Purdue and UMass Amherst.
Submit remaining 10 applications; apply for university-specific merit scholarships.
Complete CSS Profile and FAFSA (if applicable); prepare for interview.

disclaimer: This roadmap was built specifically for you based on the information you shared and current admission data available at the time of research. It is not a guarantee of admission to any university or receipt of any scholarship. Admissions involve many factors beyond any roadmap — including factors that change every year. University requirements, scholarship availability, and deadlines shift. Always verify directly with the institution.
